mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% but housing starts decline, or out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following an INDPRO increase, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal is neutralized by concurrent GDP growth > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when payroll growth exceeds 300k but unemployment remains flat, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio < 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.10 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals an imminent supply-side shock or demand collapse, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns when the contraction coincides with a FEDFUNDS cut.
